things.name: Louvre-Lens Museum
things.description: Satellite of the Paris Louvre built on a former coal mine, presenting world-class art in a stunning contemporary glass pavilion

things.name: Galerie du Temps
things.description: Panoramic timeline gallery within Louvre-Lens displaying 205 masterworks from antiquity to the 19th century in a single breathtaking hall

things.name: Parc de la Gloire
things.description: Green park surrounding the Louvre-Lens museum built on rehabilitated colliery land, with a lake and outdoor sculpture

things.name: Notre-Dame de Lorette Basilica
things.description: Towering WWI memorial basilica on Lorette Ridge overlooking 40,000 French graves, with a solemn lantern tower visible for miles

things.name: Anneau de la Mémoire
things.description: Haunting circular memorial ring at Notre-Dame de Lorette inscribed with 580,000 names of WWI soldiers from all nations, without hierarchy

things.name: Maison du Mineur
things.description: Restored miners' cottage museum depicting the daily life of a coal mining family in the early twentieth century

things.name: Stade Bollaert-Delelis
things.description: Iconic 38,000-capacity stadium of RC Lens, one of France's most atmospheric grounds surrounded by the working-class Bollaert district

things.name: Vimy Ridge Canadian National Memorial
things.description: Canada's most important WWI memorial preserving preserved trenches and tunnels where Canadians captured Vimy Ridge in April 1917

things.name: Musée des Traditions Locales
things.description: Local heritage museum chronicling the coal-mining era that shaped the culture, communities and architecture of Lens

things.name: Place Jean Jaurès
things.description: Central square of Lens with the Hotel de Ville, market days and the local brasseries beloved by RC Lens supporters
